mission.title: Install and Configure Spin on Kubernetes
mission.description: Spin is an open source developer tool for building and running serverless applications powered by WebAssembly. It simplifies the process of creating, deploying, and executing cloud microservices, making it ideal for developers looking to leverage WebAssembly technology.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpsMx Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the Spin chart.
```bash
helm repo add opsmx https://helmcharts.opsmx.com
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Spin using Helm
steps.description:
Install Spin using the Helm chart, creating a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install spin opsmx/spinnaker --namespace spin --create-namespace --version 2.2.7
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Spin pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spin
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Spin deployment to set resource limits for the containers.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment spin --namespace spin
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Spin release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall spin --namespace spin
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Spin.
```bash
kubectl delete crd --all
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Spin to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace spin
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of the Spin deployment.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace spin -o yaml > spin-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Spin
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Spin release to the latest version available in the repository.
```bash
helm upgrade spin opsmx/spinnaker --namespace spin --version 2.2.7
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Spin pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace spin
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the Spin pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace spin
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the logs for the failing pod.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace spin
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Spin service is not reachable, ensure the service is correctly configured and check the endpoints.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace spin
kubectl get endpoints --namespace spin
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Spin will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the Spin services as expected. You can verify this by checking the pod statuses and ensuring the service endpoints are correctly configured.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add opsmx https://helmcharts.opsmx.com
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment spin --namespace spin
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl on your local machine.
